eval_results.name: prd-extraction
eval_results.key_finding: All 5 extraction targets found: core features, 10k concurrent users NFR, Slack/GitHub integrations, REST API, and relational DB with audit logging

eval_results.name: template-population
eval_results.key_finding: All 6 template sections populated with no unfilled placeholders; structured markdown output

eval_results.name: module-identification
eval_results.key_finding: 5 modules identified: auth, project/task core domain, integration, API gateway, and notifications

eval_results.name: tech-stack-detection
eval_results.key_finding: PostgreSQL, Node.js/FastAPI, Redis for caching, OAuth/webhooks for integrations all detected from PRD hints

eval_results.name: output-format-compliance
eval_results.key_finding: Valid markdown with H1 title, H2 sections in logical order, consistent heading levels, and metadata header

strengths: Comprehensive PRD extraction covering features, NFRs, integrations, and constraints
All architecture template sections populated without leaving placeholders
Accurate module identification from feature descriptions
Reasonable tech stack inference from requirement hints
Clean markdown output with consistent heading structure and metadata
weaknesses: Tech stack suggestions are inferences; explicit PRD tech choices would be more accurate
Complex PRDs with conflicting requirements may need human review of generated skeleton
recommendations: Support PRD input in multiple formats (markdown, plain text, structured JSON)
Add confidence annotations to inferred tech stack choices
Provide a review checklist alongside the generated architecture document skeleton
